{"@context":"https:\/\/schema.org\/","@type":"Article","@id":"https:\/\/techpp.com\/2026\/05\/12\/claude-icons-symbols-meaning\/#Article","url":"https:\/\/techpp.com\/2026\/05\/12\/claude-icons-symbols-meaning\/","inLanguage":"en-US","mainEntityOfPage":"https:\/\/techpp.com\/2026\/05\/12\/claude-icons-symbols-meaning\/","headline":"What Do Different Icons and Symbols Mean on Claude - TechPP","description":"Confused by Claude\u2019s icons and symbols? Here\u2019s a complete guide to every button, feature, and UI element on Claude desktop and mobile apps.","articleBody":"AI companies behind popular AI models are now obsessed with coding benchmarks. Almost every model that started as chat first or image generation has slowly pivoted to coding. However, Claude initially focused on coding and is now pivoting into other categories.        Claude can now do more than just your coding tasks. Anthropic, the company behind Claude, has recently launched several features making Claude useful for most daily tasks, whether it's for coding, automation, business, or even for design.    I use Claude daily for my routine tasks. I published a detailed guide on how you can use Claude for everyday tasks. However, the biggest ambiguity for many people is that most of them are confused about how to use Claude or these new AI applications. It is understandable, since AI applications are new to the market and not many people are aware of them. Moreover, they are in the early stages and often experiment with new designs.        We have created UI guides to help with what the various features, icons, and symbols mean on the apps. We already have a detailed guide on Gemini icons, and a recently published icons guide on Perplexity. In this guide, you will learn everything about Claude icons and their meanings.  Claude Icons Explained: Meaning of Every Claude Symbol and Button  Claude uses unique names for its features and uses icons compared to the rest of the AI apps. Even I get confused most of the time. To make it easy for you, I have divided the Claude icons explanation guide into multiple sections.    First, you will learn the meanings of the various icons in the Claude web interface and in the mobile app. Each section is divided into pages to help you understand what each icon means. Before that, here is a simple icon reference.        Related: View full Claude icons guide.  Claude Desktop - Home Page  During the early days, the Claude Homepage used to be very minimal, with just the prompt box. But today, the homepage is filled with plenty of features. Here is what each icon and feature means on the Claude homepage.            Sl no  Symbol  What it does      1  New chat  You can start a new conversation with Claude.      2  Search  Let's you search your previous chats\u00a0      3  Chats  It opens your chat history. You can view all your past conversations here      4  Projects  It lets you create and manage your projects.\u00a0      5  Code  It opens Claude's code. A specialized coding environment inside Claude.      6  Customize  It lets you change or personalize how Claude responds to your questions.\u00a0      7  Design  It has recently launched Claude Design, where you can create UI designs using Claude.      8  Profile section  It shows your account details and plan. You can access all your profile-related settings here.      9  Install \/ Download button  A marketing button that prompts you to download Claude apps.        Claude\u2019s Main Prompt Box  This is where you spend most of your time. To help you understand various icons and features, here is what each icon means on Claude desktop main prompt box.            S.no  Symbol  What it does      1  Enter your prompt  This is where you type your question      2  Plus button  Lets you add or attach media to your prompt. You can learn about these options in the table below      3  Model selector  Lets you switch between different Claude models based on your needs.\u00a0      4  Voice input  Lets you speak instead of typing your prompt. It also responds to you in a voice.      5  Code  Get prompt suggestions for coding-related tasks.      6  Write  Get prompt suggestions for writing-related tasks.      7  Create  You can use this to create suggestions for creating related tasks. Example: Develop storytelling frameworks.      8  Learn  Get suggestions to help you learn something.      9  Life stuff  Get suggestions for daily life tasks. You can use it for planning, routines, advice, and personal work.        Claude Desktop Incognito Mode  Similar to other applications, Claude also offers incognito mode. Claude uses this cute octopus icon.            S.no  Symbol  What it does      1  Incognito mode  Let's start a private chat in Claude. Your conversations in incognito mode will not be saved and will not be used for training.        Claude Desktop Plus Menu Symbols and What They Do  The plus icon in the prompt bar allows you to attach media files to the Claude. You can also enable several tools such as deep research, web search, and much more.            S.no  Symbol  What it does      1  Add files or photos  It lets you upload files or images to Claude. You can select files locally from your device.      2  Take a screenshot  Capture a screenshot and add it to your chat. When you tap on the icon, it allows you to select a specific tab in which you can take a screenshot.      3  Add to project  Add the current chat to specific projects.      4  Skills  Here you can add Claude's skills or select your own skill for Claude. Skills are specialized instructions and abilities that you can use for specific tasks.      5  Add connectors  It lets you connect your Claude to external applications such as Slack, Google Drive, Gmail, and much more.      6  Research  It does more research by referring to multiple sources before answering. Deep Research consumes more tokens.      7  Web search  Allow Claude to search the internet to find the latest information.      8  Use style  Set Claude to follow a specific tone and structure for your outputs. Currently, it supports concise, explanatory, learning, and much more.        Claude Desktop More Menu Symbols and What They Do  Claude recently rolled out plenty of features, so much so that some of them are hidden under more options. Here is what they mean.            S.no  Symbol  What it does      1  Artifacts  Claude artifacts are mini apps that you build inside the Claude. These apps are mini-applications or websites run directly inside Claude. You can also manage all your Claude artifacts from here.      2  Customize sidebar  You can remove unwanted options from the Claude sidebar. For example, you can hide the new design and code options.      3  Sidebar toggle  Lets you collapse or expand the sidebar.\u00a0        Claude Desktop Chat Response Symbols and What They Do  Claude chat interface also comes with plenty of icons. Here is what they mean.            S.no  Symbol  What it does      1  Your message  This is your prompt that you sent to Claude. All your prompts appear on the right.      2  Claude response  Shows Claude\u2019s answer to your question. All of Claude's answers appear on the left.\u00a0      3  Copy response  Let's you copy Claude\u2019s answer.\u00a0      4  Like response  Let's give positive feedback when the answer is useful.      5  Dislike response  Let's give feedback when the answer is not useful or not correct.      6  Retry response  Regenerate the answer if you don't like the output. It also allows you to view the previous response.      7  Chat title menu  This is the chat title menu where you can rename, star it, or add it to any project.        Claude Mobile  1. Claude Mobile Home page  Unlike the desktop or web version, the mobile version is less cluttered and minimal interface. It still has plenty of options, which can confuse users. Here is what each icon and feature means.            S.no  Symbol  What it does      1  Menu button  It opens the main Claude sidebar menu on top. From here, you can access chats, projects, settings, and other options from here.      2  Incognito mode  You can enable Incognito Mode in Claude using this icon. All your conversations will not be saved and will not be tracked.\u00a0      3  Main prompt box  This is where you type your prompt for Claude.      4  Plus button  The plus icon gives you more options to customize your prompt. You can attach media, enable search, and much more.      5  Voice input  Let's you speak your prompt instead of typing it.      6  Voice mode  Opens voice conversation mode. You can talk with Claude directly instead of using normal text chat. It also replaces you in the voice mode.        Claude Mobile: Add to chat Symbols and What They Do  The Plus interface on Claude mobile is neatly aligned with an easy-to-access tools. On the top, you can find all the file options you can attach to the prompt, and at the bottom, you can find all the other tools.            S.no  Symbol  What it does      1  Camera  Attach photos to your Claude by taking shots directly from the camera      2  Photos  You can upload an image from your phone's gallery      3  Files  Let's you upload a file from your phone      4  Web search  Let Claude search the web to answer your questions with the best, latest, and most accurate answers      5  Add to project  Let's you add the chat to a project      6  Use style  Let you choose a writing style for Claude\u2019s response. Currently, you can select options that include concise, learning, short, and much more      7  Tool access  Customize how Claude decides which tools to load. Currently offers three options. Auto is where Claude decides which tools to load based on the task, and On Demand, where Claude only loads the tool when necessary, and finally, always available, which preloads all the tools before you even chat.      8  Connectors  Allow Claude to connect to the external apps. You can browse and add multiple connectors.        Claude Mobile Sidebar Symbols and What They Do  The sidebar on the mobile also comes with plenty of options. Here is what each icon means.            S.no  Symbol  What it does      1  New chat  Start a new chat with Claude      2  Chats  Opens your chat history. You can view and manage all your old chats there.\u00a0      3  Projects  Let's you create and manage projects. Projects are similar to folders where you can store all your chats, resources, and reference files in one place.      4  Artifacts  Opens Claude Artifacts. Claude Artifacts are small mini-apps that you build inside Claude.      5  Profile section  This is your profile picture and your username. You can tap to go to settings.\u00a0      6  Settings  Opens the Claude app main settings.\u00a0        Claude Mobile Chat Symbols and What They Do  The chat interface on Claude mobile comes with a few extra options, such as Read aloud. Here is what all icons mean.            S.no  Symbol  What it does      1  User message  Shows all your messages on the right side.      2  Back button  Takes you back to the home screen.      3  More options  Opens more options for the current chat. It consists of options such as rename, star, add to home, and delete, and more.\u00a0      4  Copy response  Let's you copy Claude\u2019s answer.\u00a0      5  Share response  Let's share Claude\u2019s response with others. When you share, it shares the entire conversation instead of individual messages.      6  Read aloud  Convert text output into voice.      7  Like response  Let's give positive feedback if the answer is useful.      8  Dislike response  Give feedback when the answer is not useful or not accurate.\u00a0          9  Retry response  Regenerate the answer if you don\u2019t like the current output        Claude Mobile Settings Symbols and What They Do  Claude offers plenty of options on desktop, while on mobile, they are limited. So here is what the features and icons mean in the Claude mobile app.            Number  Symbol  What it does      1  Profile  It opens your profile screen, where you can edit your full name and change the name by which Claude should call you, and also provide personalized instructions. You can also delete your account from here.      2  Billing  If you are a premium subscriber, you can manage all your billing from here.      3  Capabilities  Capabilities are Claude's abilities to perform and use different tools. You can manage all Claude capabilities in a single place, including whether or not to let Claude web search, code execution, generate memory from chat, access tools, and much more.      4  Connectors  Connect Claude to external apps such as Gmail, Google Calendar, Notion, and Slack.      5  Permissions  Manage Claude's permissions from here.      6  Color mode  You can change the theme of the Claude app. Currently supports light and dark modes.      7  Font style  Change the font style. You can select the default app font or the system font.      8  Speech language  Select the language in which Claude wants to reply to you.      9  Haptic feedback  It turns on vibration every time Claude responds to your questions. You can turn it off to save battery life.      10  Privacy  It lets you view data privacy and allows you to opt out of using your chats and coding sessions to train AI models      11  Shared links  Manage all your Claude shared chat links here\u00a0      12  Info button  View more privacy and terms, and also see your Claude app version number.\u00a0        Claude is Easier to Understand Once You Know the UI  Those are the meanings of icons and features in the Claude web and mobile app. Claude Web on the desktop interfaces has a very similar experience, so knowing Claude Web will also make you familiar with the Claude desktop interface.    Like the new AI application, Claude uses very unique icons, which can be confusing for beginners. You can treat this post as a Claude Legend and Icons Guide.    Since Claude is experimenting with new features every week and also launching new ones, I frequently update this post to stay updated and help you navigate Claude easily on all platforms. If you are still confused, let me know in the comments below, and I will be happy to help you.  FAQs on the Meaning of Claude Icons  1. Does Claude keep changing its icons and interface often?  These AI tools are new to the market, and Claude is launching new features every single day. While most of the UI and elements will remain similar, you will find new additions every day until all these AI applications find their final functional design.  2. Why do some Claude icons appear on desktop but not on mobile?  Anthropic, the company behind Claude, intentionally limits some features on mobile to make it less cluttered for the user, and moreover, some features are restricted because of the mobile limitations.  3. How can I know what an icon does before clicking it?  There are no tool tips to help you know what each icon does in Claude. However, you can download our legend and icon guide as a reference.  4. Why does my Claude interface look different from the screenshots in this guide?  Some of the latest versions of the Claude might look different than the screenshots used in this post. However, we update this post regularly. If the interface still looks different, let me know in the comments, and I will help you.  5. What is the easiest way to navigate Claude as a beginner?  Even though Claude is new, most features are easy to understand, and the interface is beginner-friendly. You can view our guide for reference.   --bricks-color-scnbdd: #f4f7fa;
  --desktop-gap: 32px;
  --mobile-gap: 24px;
  --small-gap: 16px;
  --black: #000000;
  --dm-bg: #121212;
  --dm-text: #c9d1d9;
  --dm-bg-blue: #12122c;
  --dm-bg-lightDM: #555555;
}
/* Global Variable Declaration */

a {
  text-decoration-thickness: 2px !important;
  text-underline-offset: 2px !important;
}

img {
  background-color: var(--bricks-color-scnbdd);
  border-radius: 2px;
}
code {
    background-color: var(--bricks-bg-light);
    color: #fc1f49;
    font-size: 13px;
    margin: 0;
    padding: 10px 15px;
    white-space: pre-wrap;
}

.code-block-label {
  font-family: "Jost";
  font-style: normal;
  font-weight: 400;
  font-size: 14px;
  text-align: center;
  color: #c0c0c0;
}
.bricks-button {
  padding: 12px 24px;
}

body.bricks-is-frontend :focus {
    outline: none;
}

/* DarkMode Colors */

html[data-theme="dark"], [data-theme="dark"] body {
  background-color: var(--dm-bg) !important;
  color: var(--dm-text);
}
[data-theme="dark"] .bg-white {
  background-color: var(--dm-bg) !important;
}
[data-theme="dark"] .bg-black {
  background-color: var(--dm-text) !important;

    <style type="text/css">/* Multi-row tables */
    @media (max-width: 727px) {

        .mtr-table tbody,
        .mtr-table {
        	width: 100% !important;
            display: table !important;
        }

        .mtr-table tr,
        .mtr-table .mtr-th-tag,
        .mtr-table .mtr-td-tag {
        	display: block !important;
            clear: both !important;
            height: auto !important;
        }

        .mtr-table .mtr-td-tag,
        .mtr-table .mtr-th-tag {
        	text-align: right !important;
            width: auto !important;
            box-sizing: border-box !important;
            overflow: auto !important;
        }
    
            .mtr-table .mtr-cell-content {
            	text-align: right !important;
            }
            
        .mtr-table tbody,
        .mtr-table tr,
        .mtr-table {
        	border: none !important;
            padding: 0 !important;
        }

        .mtr-table .mtr-td-tag,
        .mtr-table .mtr-th-tag {
        	border: none;
        }

        .mtr-table tr:nth-child(even) .mtr-td-tag,
        .mtr-table tr:nth-child(even) .mtr-th-tag {
            border-bottom: 1px solid #dddddd !important;
            border-left: 1px solid #dddddd !important;
            border-right: 1px solid #dddddd !important;
            border-top: none !important;
        }

        .mtr-table tr:nth-child(odd) .mtr-td-tag,
        .mtr-table tr:nth-child(odd) .mtr-th-tag {
            border-bottom: 1px solid #bbbbbb !important;
            border-left: 1px solid #bbbbbb !important;
            border-right: 1px solid #bbbbbb !important;
            border-top: none !important;
        }

        .mtr-table tr:first-of-type td:first-of-type,
        .mtr-table tr:first-of-type th:first-of-type {
            border-top: 1px solid #bbbbbb !important;
        }

        .mtr-table.mtr-thead-td tr:nth-of-type(2) td:first-child,
        .mtr-table.mtr-thead-td tr:nth-of-type(2) th:first-child,
        .mtr-table.mtr-tr-th tr:nth-of-type(2) td:first-child,
        .mtr-table.mtr-tr-th tr:nth-of-type(2) th:first-child,
        .mtr-table.mtr-tr-td tr:nth-of-type(2) td:first-child,
        .mtr-table.mtr-tr-td tr:nth-of-type(2) th:first-child {
            border-top: 1px solid #dddddd !important;
        }
            .mtr-table tr:nth-child(even),
            .mtr-table tr:nth-child(even) .mtr-td-tag,
            .mtr-table tr:nth-child(even) .mtr-th-tag {
                background: #ffffff !important;
            }

            .mtr-table tr:nth-child(odd),
            .mtr-table tr:nth-child(odd) .mtr-td-tag,
            .mtr-table tr:nth-child(odd) .mtr-th-tag {
                background: #dddddd !important;
            }
        .mtr-table .mtr-td-tag,
        .mtr-table .mtr-td-tag:first-child,
        .mtr-table .mtr-th-tag,
        .mtr-table .mtr-th-tag:first-child {
            padding: 5px 10px !important;
        }
        
        .mtr-table td[data-mtr-content]:before,
        .mtr-table th[data-mtr-content]:before {
        	display: inline-block !important;
        	content: attr(data-mtr-content) !important;
        	float: left !important;
            text-align: left !important;
            white-space: pre-line !important;
        }
        
        .mtr-table thead,
        .mtr-table.mtr-tr-th tr:first-of-type,
        .mtr-table.mtr-tr-td tr:first-of-type,
        .mtr-table colgroup {
        	display: none !important;
        }
    
        .mtr-cell-content {
            max-width: 49% !important;
            display: inline-block !important;
        }
            
        .mtr-table td[data-mtr-content]:before,
        .mtr-table th[data-mtr-content]:before {
            max-width: 49% !important;
        }
            
    }
    
    /* Single-row tables */
    @media (max-width: 650px) {

        .mtr-one-row-table .mtr-td-tag,
        .mtr-one-row-table .mtr-th-tag {
            box-sizing: border-box !important;
        }

        .mtr-one-row-table colgroup {
        	display: none !important;
        }

    
        .mtr-one-row-table tbody,
        .mtr-one-row-table tr,
        .mtr-one-row-table {
        	border: none !important;
            padding: 0 !important;
            width: 100% !important;
            display: block;
        }

        .mtr-one-row-table .mtr-td-tag,
        .mtr-one-row-table .mtr-th-tag {
            border: none;
        }
        
        .mtr-one-row-table .mtr-td-tag,
        .mtr-one-row-table .mtr-th-tag {
        	display: block !important;
            width: 50% !important;
        }
        .mtr-one-row-table tr {
            display: flex !important;
            flex-wrap: wrap !important;
        }
        
            .mtr-one-row-table td:nth-child(4n+1),
            .mtr-one-row-table th:nth-child(4n+1),
            .mtr-one-row-table td:nth-child(4n+2),
            .mtr-one-row-table th:nth-child(4n+2) {
                background: #dddddd !important;
            }

            .mtr-one-row-table th:nth-child(2n+1),
            .mtr-one-row-table td:nth-child(2n+1) {
                border-right: 1px solid #bbbbbb !important;
                border-left: 1px solid #bbbbbb !important;
            }

            .mtr-one-row-table td:nth-child(2n+2),
            .mtr-one-row-table th:nth-child(2n+2) {
                border-right: 1px solid #bbbbbb !important;
            }

            /* last two */
            .mtr-one-row-table td:nth-last-child(-n+2),
            .mtr-one-row-table th:nth-last-child(-n+2) {
                border-bottom: 1px solid #bbbbbb !important;
            }
            
        .mtr-one-row-table .mtr-td-tag,
        .mtr-one-row-table .mtr-th-tag {
            border-top: 1px solid #bbbbbb !important;
            padding: 5px 10px !important;
        }
        
    }</style>
    <!-- END - Make Tables Responsive -->

<div class="summary-box">
  <h3 class="highlights-heading bg-white ds-text">
    Quick Answer  </h3>
  <div id="article-highlights">
    <p>Claude’s icons and symbols help you navigate chats, projects, coding tools, voice mode, web search, artifacts, and settings across desktop and mobile. Common icons include New Chat, Plus for attachments, Incognito Mode, Web Search, Retry Response, and Artifacts for mini apps.</p>
  </div>
</div>

</div><div id="brxe-srgmbb" class="brxe-post-content post-content"><p>AI companies behind popular AI models are now obsessed with coding benchmarks. Almost every model that started as chat first or image generation has slowly pivoted to coding. However, Claude initially focused on coding and is now pivoting into other categories.</p>
<p><a href="https://techpp.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/What-does-claude-code-symbols-mean.jpg"><img decoding="async" class="aligncenter size-full wp-image-253804" src="https://techpp.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/What-does-claude-code-symbols-mean.jpg" alt="What does claude code symbols mean" width="1200" height="625" loading="eager" fetchpriority="high"></a></p>
<p>Claude can now do more than just your coding tasks. Anthropic, the company behind Claude, has recently launched several features making Claude useful for most daily tasks, whether it’s for coding, automation, business, or even for design.</p>
<p>I use Claude daily for my routine tasks. I published a <a href="https://techpp.com/2026/03/05/claude-cowork-use-cases/">detailed guide on how you can use Claude for everyday tasks</a>. However, the biggest ambiguity for many people is that most of them are confused about how to use Claude or these new AI applications. It is understandable, since AI applications are new to the market and not many people are aware of them. Moreover, they are in the early stages and often experiment with new designs.</p>
<div class="code-block code-block-6 code-block-block-6" style="margin: 8px auto; text-align: center; display: block; clear: both; min-height: 330px; width: 100%;" data-warp-lrc-idx="5">
<div class="no-visibility-check ai-check-6-86408062" data-insertion-position="after" data-selector=".ai-check-6-86408062" data-code="" data-block="6"><span class="ai-check-block ai-cfp" data-ai-block="6" data-ai-max-clicks="3" data-ai-limit-clicks-per-time="2" data-ai-limit-clicks-time="1" data-ai-hash="50849" data-ai-cfp-time="1"></span></div>

</div>

<p>We have created UI guides to help with what the various features, icons, and symbols mean on the apps. We already have a <a href="https://techpp.com/2026/02/23/google-gemini-icons-explained/">detailed guide on Gemini icons</a>, and a recently published <a href="https://techpp.com/2026/04/15/perplexity-icons-explained/">icons guide on Perplexity</a>. In this guide, you will learn everything about Claude icons and their meanings.</p>

<h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Claude_Icons_Explained_Meaning_of_Every_Claude_Symbol_and_Button"></span>Claude Icons Explained: Meaning of Every Claude Symbol and Button<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2>
<p>Claude uses unique names for its features and uses icons compared to the rest of the AI apps. Even I get confused most of the time. To make it easy for you, I have divided the Claude icons explanation guide into multiple sections.</p>
<p>First, you will learn the meanings of the various icons in the Claude web interface and in the mobile app. Each section is divided into pages to help you understand what each icon means. Before that, here is a simple icon reference.</p>
<p><a href="https://techpp.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Claude-icons-guide.jpg"><img decoding="async" class="aligncenter size-full wp-image-253811" src="https://techpp.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Claude-icons-guide.jpg" alt="Claude icons guide" width="1169" height="1535" loading="eager"></a></p>
<p><strong>Related:</strong> <a href="https://techpp.app/explained/claude-icon-guide" target="_blank" rel="noopener">View full Claude icons guide</a>.</p>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Claude_Desktop_%E2%80%93_Home_Page"></span>Claude Desktop – Home Page<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p>During the early days, the Claude Homepage used to be very minimal, with just the prompt box. But today, the homepage is filled with plenty of features. Here is what each icon and feature means on the Claude homepage.</p>
<p><a href="https://techpp.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Claude-Sidebar-Icons-and-What-They-Do-1.jpg"><img decoding="async" class="aligncenter size-full wp-image-253764" src="https://techpp.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Claude-Sidebar-Icons-and-What-They-Do-1.jpg" alt="Claude Sidebar Icons and What They Do" width="1450" height="906" loading="eager"></a></p>
<table data-mtr-unique-id="4b0aca0af0419a9e91a2ae19900728af-1">
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Sl no</strong></td>
<td><strong>Symbol</strong></td>
<td><strong>What it does</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">1</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">New chat</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">You can start a new conversation with Claude.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">2</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Search</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Let’s you search your previous chats </span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">3</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Chats</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">It opens your chat history. You can view all your past conversations here</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">4</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Projects</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">It lets you create and manage your projects. </span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">5</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Code</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">It opens Claude’s code. A specialized coding environment inside Claude.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">6</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Customize</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">It lets you change or personalize how Claude responds to your questions. </span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">7</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Design</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">It has recently launched Claude Design, where you can create UI designs using Claude.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">8</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Profile section</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">It shows your account details and plan. You can access all your profile-related settings here.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">9</span></td>

<th data-mtr-content="S.no" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">4</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="Symbol" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Copy response</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="What it does" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Let’s you copy Claude’s answer. </span></div></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th data-mtr-content="S.no" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">5</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="Symbol" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Share response</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="What it does" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Let’s share Claude’s response with others. When you share, it shares the entire conversation instead of individual messages.</span></div></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th data-mtr-content="S.no" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">6</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="Symbol" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Read aloud</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="What it does" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Convert text output into voice.</span></div></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th data-mtr-content="S.no" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">7</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="Symbol" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Like response</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="What it does" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Let’s give positive feedback if the answer is useful.</span></div></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th data-mtr-content="S.no" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">8</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="Symbol" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Dislike response</span></div></th>
<th data-mtr-content="What it does" class="mtr-th-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Give feedback when the answer is not useful or not accurate. </span></div></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td data-mtr-content="S.no" class="mtr-td-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">9</span></div></td>
<td data-mtr-content="Symbol" class="mtr-td-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Retry response</span></div></td>
<td data-mtr-content="What it does" class="mtr-td-tag"><div class="mtr-cell-content"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Regenerate the answer if you don’t like the current output</span></div></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Claude_Mobile_Settings_Symbols_and_What_They_Do"></span>Claude Mobile Settings Symbols and What They Do<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p>Claude offers plenty of options on desktop, while on mobile, they are limited. So here is what the features and icons mean in the Claude mobile app.</p>
<p><a href="https://techpp.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Claude-Mobile-Settings-Symbols-and-What-They-Do.jpg"><img decoding="async" class="aligncenter size-full wp-image-253784" src="https://techpp.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/05/Claude-Mobile-Settings-Symbols-and-What-They-Do.jpg" alt="Claude Mobile Settings Symbols and What They Do" width="2000" height="1250" loading="lazy" fetchpriority="low"></a></p>
<table data-mtr-unique-id="4b0aca0af0419a9e91a2ae19900728af-11">
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><strong>Number</strong></td>
<td><strong>Symbol</strong></td>
<td><strong>What it does</strong></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">1</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Profile</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">It opens your profile screen, where you can edit your full name and change the name by which Claude should call you, and also provide personalized instructions. You can also delete your account from here.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">2</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Billing</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">If you are a premium subscriber, you can manage all your billing from here.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">3</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Capabilities</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Capabilities are Claude’s abilities to perform and use different tools. You can manage all Claude capabilities in a single place, including whether or not to let Claude web search, code execution, generate memory from chat, access tools, and much more.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">4</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Connectors</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Connect Claude to external apps such as Gmail, Google Calendar, Notion, and Slack.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">5</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Permissions</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Manage Claude’s permissions from here.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">6</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Color mode</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">You can change the theme of the Claude app. Currently supports light and dark modes.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">7</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Font style</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Change the font style. You can select the default app font or the system font.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">8</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Speech language</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Select the language in which Claude wants to reply to you.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">9</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Haptic feedback</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">It turns on vibration every time Claude responds to your questions. You can turn it off to save battery life.</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">10</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Privacy</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">It lets you view data privacy and allows you to opt out of using your chats and coding sessions to train AI models</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">11</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Shared links</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Manage all your Claude shared chat links here </span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">12</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">Info button</span></td>
<td><span style="font-weight: 400;">View more privacy and terms, and also see your Claude app version number. </span></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="Claude_is_Easier_to_Understand_Once_You_Know_the_UI"></span>Claude is Easier to Understand Once You Know the UI<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p>Those are the meanings of icons and features in the Claude web and mobile app. Claude Web on the desktop interfaces has a very similar experience, so knowing Claude Web will also make you familiar with the Claude desktop interface.</p>
<p>Like the new AI application, Claude uses very unique icons, which can be confusing for beginners. You can treat this post as a Claude Legend and Icons Guide.</p>
<p>Since Claude is experimenting with new features every week and also launching new ones, I frequently update this post to stay updated and help you navigate Claude easily on all platforms. If you are still confused, let me know in the comments below, and I will be happy to help you.</p>
<h2><span class="ez-toc-section" id="FAQs_on_the_Meaning_of_Claude_Icons"></span>FAQs on the Meaning of Claude Icons<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h2>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="1_Does_Claude_keep_changing_its_icons_and_interface_often"></span>1. Does Claude keep changing its icons and interface often?<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<div class="code-block code-block-3 code-block-block-3" style="margin: 8px auto; text-align: center; display: block; clear: both; min-height: 330px; width: 100%;" data-warp-lrc-idx="5">
<div class="no-visibility-check ai-check-3-93926416" data-insertion-position="after" data-selector=".ai-check-3-93926416" data-code="" data-block="3"><span class="ai-check-block ai-cfp" data-ai-block="3" data-ai-max-clicks="3" data-ai-limit-clicks-per-time="2" data-ai-limit-clicks-time="1" data-ai-hash="27313" data-ai-cfp-time="1"></span></div>

</div>
<p>These AI tools are new to the market, and Claude is launching new features every single day. While most of the UI and elements will remain similar, you will find new additions every day until all these AI applications find their final functional design.</p>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="2_Why_do_some_Claude_icons_appear_on_desktop_but_not_on_mobile"></span>2. Why do some Claude icons appear on desktop but not on mobile?<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p>Anthropic, the company behind Claude, intentionally limits some features on mobile to make it less cluttered for the user, and moreover, some features are restricted because of the mobile limitations.</p>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="3_How_can_I_know_what_an_icon_does_before_clicking_it"></span>3. How can I know what an icon does before clicking it?<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p>There are no tool tips to help you know what each icon does in Claude. However, you can download our legend and icon guide as a reference.</p>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="4_Why_does_my_Claude_interface_look_different_from_the_screenshots_in_this_guide"></span>4. Why does my Claude interface look different from the screenshots in this guide?<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p>Some of the latest versions of the Claude might look different than the screenshots used in this post. However, we update this post regularly. If the interface still looks different, let me know in the comments, and I will help you.</p>
<h3><span class="ez-toc-section" id="5_What_is_the_easiest_way_to_navigate_Claude_as_a_beginner"></span>5. What is the easiest way to navigate Claude as a beginner?<span class="ez-toc-section-end"></span></h3>
<p>Even though Claude is new, most features are easy to understand, and the interface is beginner-friendly. You can view our guide for reference. Once you understand the interface, it becomes easier to navigate.</p>
